top_signals.title: Denmark and Germany Launch Europe’s First Hydrogen Superhighway
top_signals.summary: For the past few years, hydrogen has suffered from a credibility problem. Not because the technology itself stopped making sense, but because expectations ran too far ahead of reality. Governments announced hydrogen strategies worth hundreds of billions of euros. Companies unveiled ambitious projects stretching from Portugal to Poland. Analysts forecast a future hydrogen economy that would decarbonize everything from steel and chemicals to shipping and aviation. Then came the inevitable correction. Costs remained higher than expected. Financing…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: New York, Maryland and Utah to hold primaries with crypto PAC money hanging over voters
top_signals.summary: Crypto-backed PACs disclosed spending more than $8 million on media to support candidates in three US states, as some Maryland Democrats have called on one candidate to reject “outside spending from crypto billionaires.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Japan aims to double proportion of female engineering students by 2040
top_signals.summary: In 2025, the share of female university students in engineering faculties stood at 18%. The government hopes to boost the figure to 36% in 2040.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
top_signals.summary: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credentials. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Body thought to have passed 15 years since death found in freezer
top_signals.summary: An autopsy revealed that the person, who lived in the apartment where his severed body was found, died around December 2011.
